problem_statement: When organizations automate decision-making with ML, 'human-in-the-loop' often becomes a compliance label where reviewers approve outputs without the authority, information, time, or confidence to challenge them, creating a false sense of oversight while outcomes remain effectively automated.
insufficiency_evidence: many AI workflows involve human reviewers who formally approve model outputs without the authority, information, time, or confidence to challenge them meaningfully; 'review' effectively becomes approval by default, especially for borderline declines; near-zero override rate in high-stakes decisions
solution_mechanisms: Override control (decision rights to change outcomes)
Execution Boundary Control (intervene before irreversible action)
KPI measurement framework (override rate, escalation precision, latency, disagreement, harm signals)
notes: Document addresses automated decision-making broadly (credit, hiring, healthcare) rather than AI-generated code specifically; oversight framework is domain-general but transferable.
